title: Forms and Input Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential tags for creating interactive HTML forms.

questions.question: Which tag acts as a container for all elements in a form?
What is the purpose of the 'action' attribute in the <form> tag?
options: It determines the visual appearance of the form.
It specifies the URL where the form data should be sent.
It defines the HTTP method to use (GET or POST).
It specifies a primary heading for the form.

questions.question: Which input type should you use to capture a password securely (masking characters)?
How do you correctly link a <label> to an <input> tag for better accessibility?
options: By placing the input inside the label.
By using the same text for both.
By matching the label's 'for' attribute with the input's 'id' attribute.
By setting the 'name' attribute of both to the same value.

questions.question: Which element should you use for a multi-line text input (like a message)?
